briefs.title: Council to vote on higher development fees and a rezoning in Port Coquitlam
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam Council is set to vote on two bylaws at its April 14, 2026 meeting. The first would formally adopt updated Development Cost Charges (DCCs) — one-time fees charged to developers to help pay for parks, roads, and utilities needed as the city grows. The second would finalize a rezoning at 1806 Pitt River Road, allowing one lot to be split into two smaller residential lots.
briefs.action_required: If you are developing in Port Coquitlam, check the new DCC fee schedule — costs have risen significantly.

key_dates.label: Port Coquitlam DCC Amendment Bylaw (Parks) adoption vote — council meeting
Port Coquitlam Zoning Amendment — 1806 Pitt River Road adoption vote

briefs.title: Council to consider: Port Coquitlam adopts April 14, 2026 meeting agenda
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam Council is set to vote on formally adopting the agenda for its April 14, 2026 meeting. This is a routine procedural step that confirms which items will be discussed and decided at that session — it does not itself change any rules or bylaws.

briefs.title: Council to Approve March 10 Minutes: Zoning Changes Advance for Two Port Coquitlam Properties
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam Council is set to vote on adopting the minutes from its March 10, 2026 meeting. Those minutes record that Council gave first three readings — an early but important step toward passing — to zoning amendment bylaws for two properties: 3473 Oxford Street and 3311 Handley Crescent. These proposed zoning changes are not yet final and must meet additional conditions before they can be fully adopted.
briefs.action_required: If you own or operate near 3473 Oxford St or 3311 Handley Crescent, monitor these zoning changes closely.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Port Coquitlam Proclaiming April 28 as Day of Mourning
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam Mayor Brad West is set to formally proclaim April 28, 2026 as the 'Day of Mourning' — a national day recognized by the Canadian Labour Congress to honour workers killed, injured, or made ill on the job. The proclamation also calls for stronger workplace health and safety standards across Canada.
briefs.action_required: Mark April 28, 2026 on your calendar to recognize workplace safety and reflect on your own safety obligations.

key_dates.label: Port Coquitlam Day of Mourning proclamation — workplace safety awareness date

briefs.industries: Labour & Employment, Construction & Development, Natural Resources

briefs.title: Council to vote on adopting updated Development Cost Charges for parks in Port Coquitlam
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam Council is set to vote on formally adopting an amendment to its Development Cost Charge (DCC) bylaw — a fee that developers pay to help fund public infrastructure like parks. The amendment has already received provincial approval and has gone through multiple readings since January 2026, and Council will now decide whether to make it official.
briefs.action_required: If you develop property in Port Coquitlam, review the updated DCC fee schedule before your next project budget.

key_dates.label: Port Coquitlam DCC Amendment Bylaw No. 4431 — Council adoption vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on final approval of rezoning at 1806 Pitt River Road
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam Council is set to vote on the final adoption of a zoning amendment bylaw that would officially rezone 1806 Pitt River Road from RS1 (single-family residential) to RS4 (a different single-family zone that allows for subdivision into smaller lots). All required conditions — including tree protection fencing, demolition of existing structures, and submission of fees for off-site work — have been confirmed as met.
briefs.action_required: Watch the April 14, 2026 council meeting if this rezoning affects your property or business plans.

key_dates.label: Port Coquitlam rezoning bylaw 4338 — final adoption council vote

briefs.summary: The agenda item listed as 'Reports' for the Port Coquitlam Council meeting on April 14, 2026 contains no body text or details. There is nothing to summarize or act on at this time.

briefs.title: Council to vote on higher development fees for parks in Port Coquitlam
briefs.summary: Port Coquitlam Council is set to vote on April 14, 2026 to formally adopt an updated Development Cost Charge (DCC) bylaw — a one-time fee charged to developers when they build — that adds a new city-wide parks fee and significantly raises total charges. For example, the total DCC on a single-family home would rise from roughly $15,762 to $31,015, nearly doubling.
briefs.action_required: If you have a development application in progress, check whether you qualify for 12-month in-stream rate protection before the bylaw is adopted.

key_dates.label: Port Coquitlam Parks DCC Amendment Bylaw adoption vote — council meeting
